name: rest-countries
title: REST Countries
description: Search and explore detailed data for every country: flags, capitals, languages, currencies, regions and more, powered by the REST Countries API.
author: yusifaliyevpro

preferences.name: apiKey
preferences.title: API Key
preferences.description: Your REST Countries API key. Get one for free at https://restcountries.com/sign-up.

preferences.name: entriesWithoutIsoCode
preferences.title: Show Countries
preferences.description: REST Countries lists a few partially-recognized territories that have no ISO 3166-1 code (for example Abkhazia, South Ossetia and Northern Cyprus). Choose whether to include them. This is based purely on the data source's ISO codes and is not a political stance by this extension or its author.

commands.name: search-countries
commands.title: Search Countries
commands.subtitle: REST Countries
commands.description: Search countries by name, capital, or language and browse rich details like flags, currencies, and regions.

tools.name: find-countries
tools.title: Find Countries
tools.description: Find countries by a free-text query such as a name, capital, language, currency, or top-level domain.

tools.name: get-country
tools.title: Get Country
tools.description: Get details about a single country by name, ISO code, or capital city.

tools.name: list-countries
tools.title: List Countries
tools.description: List countries by criteria like region, subregion, language, currency, landlocked status, or organisation membership.

ai.instructions: This extension provides data about countries from the REST Countries API. Use 'get-country' for questions about one specific country, 'list-countries' when the user asks by region, subregion, language, currency, landlocked status, or an organisation like the EU/NATO/UN, and 'find-countries' for free-text lookups (e.g. by capital city, demonym, or top-level domain like '.ca'). Region must be one of: Africa, Americas, Antarctic, Asia, Europe, Oceania. Make as few tool calls as possible to conserve the user's API quota: issue a single tool call, wait for its result, and only call another tool if that result is genuinely insufficient. Never run the same or overlapping queries in parallel or as a fallback — pick the single best tool and query for the question. When calling a tool, only pass the parameters the user actually asked for; never invent or default extra filters (for example, don't add 'landlocked: false' to a request for EU members). Every tool requires a 'fields' parameter listing the country properties to return — always request the minimal set that answers the question (e.g. ['names','capitals'] for a capital) and include 'names' so results stay identifiable. The list tools are paginated and return a 'meta' object — fetch only ONE page and answer from it; do NOT loop through pages to gather everything. If 'meta.more' is true, tell the user the total count and that they can ask for more, and only then fetch the next page with a higher 'offset'. When listing many countries, summarize with name, flag, and capital unless the user asks for more detail.

evals.input: @rest-countries what is the capital of Japan?

expected.meetsCriteria: States that the capital of Japan is Tokyo

evals.input: @rest-countries which country has Canberra as its capital?

scripts.prepublishOnly:
echo "

It seems like you are trying to publish the Raycast extension to npm.

If you did intend to publish it to npm, remove the \`prepublishOnly\` script and rerun \`npm publish\` again.
If you wanted to publish it to the Raycast Store instead, use \`npm run publish\` instead.

" && exit 1
